symbols: divine light from Child — Christ as Light of the World
angels above — heavenly host praising God
ox and donkey — traditional nativity animals
humble stable — poverty of the Incarnation
kneeling shepherds — the lowly receiving revelation first
description: A grand French Baroque Nativity scene. The Virgin Mary kneels at center beside the radiant Christ Child, who lies in a manger emanating supernatural light that illuminates the entire scene. Joseph stands nearby. Shepherds gather from both sides in attitudes of wonder and adoration. A host of angels fills the sky above, some playing instruments, others in attitudes of worship. The stable setting includes columns and architectural elements. The composition is richly detailed with a dramatic contrast between the dark night setting and the radiant light from the Christ Child.
theological_description: This grand Nativity depicts the shepherds' adoration from Luke 2:8-20, combined with the angelic proclamation. The divine light emanating from the Infant Jesus visually expresses John's Gospel: 'The true light, which gives light to everyone, was coming into the world' (John 1:9). The angels in glory above represent the heavenly host who sang 'Glory to God in the highest, and on earth peace among those with whom he is pleased' (Luke 2:14). The contrast between the humble stable and the spectacular heavenly display captures the paradox of the Incarnation — the Creator of the universe entering creation as a helpless infant, the King of Kings born in a manger.

scripture_refs.reason: Shepherds receiving the angelic announcement and visiting the manger
Glory to God in the highest — the angels' proclamation shown above
The true light coming into the world, shown as radiance from the Christ Child
The people walking in darkness have seen a great light
